linting.notes: Pre-commit hooks enforce many additional checks: license headers, YAML/JSON validation, custom security checks (FIPS compliance, SQL injection prevention), code generation checks, and API conformance. Full pre-commit can be run with 'pre-commit run --all-files' but requires Node.js, oasdiff, and other tools.

env_vars: PYTHONPATH can be set to include repo root for script execution
testing.mock_patterns: pytest-socket blocks network by default for unit tests; @pytest.mark.allow_network marker permits specific tests to use network
testing.coverage_command: uv run --python 3.12 --with-editable . --group unit coverage run --source=src/llama_stack -m pytest -s -v tests/unit/
testing.notes: Unit tests are in tests/unit/, integration tests in tests/integration/. Some unit tests require optional dependencies (torch, qdrant-client, llama-stack-client) which are in the 'unit' dependency group. The --with-editable flag is important for proper module resolution. Integration tests require infrastructure (Ollama, Docker, etc.) and run in replay mode in CI. Tests use pytest asyncio_mode=auto.

ci.notes: The ci-status workflow aggregates all GitHub Actions checks and is the final gating check. Integration tests run in multiple configurations (library, docker, server) with different Python versions and client versions. Pre-commit workflow skips 'no-commit-to-branch' and 'mypy' hooks (mypy runs separately with full type_checking group). Tests on pull_request, merge_group, and push to main/release branches.

build.build_command: No explicit build needed for library development
build.notes: Project uses uv (>=0.7.0) as package manager. The package is installed in editable mode during development. Uses setuptools-scm for versioning from git tags. Python >=3.12 required. Some dependencies (torch, torchvision) are fetched from PyTorch CPU index. The uv.lock file pins all dependencies.

conventions.test_naming: test_* functions, Test* classes for pytest
conventions.import_style: Ruff enforces import ordering (I rule) - stdlib, third-party, first-party. No star imports except in API __init__.py files.
conventions.notes: Coverage config (.coveragerc) excludes tests/, providers/, templates/, UI code, and __init__.py files. Pytest configured with asyncio_mode=auto, so no @pytest.mark.asyncio needed. Custom markers include 'allow_network' for tests that need network access. Code must be FIPS-compliant (no md5, sha1). SQL queries must use parameterized bind variables, not f-strings. Logging must use llama_stack.log, not direct logging imports.

lint_commands.notes: Found 1 fixable import ordering issue in scripts/generate_prompt_format.py (exit code 1, expected for repos with violations)

test_commands.notes: 1663 passed, 4 skipped (exit code 0). Must exclude tests requiring torch/qdrant: --ignore=tests/unit/providers/utils/inference/test_transformers_inference.py --ignore=tests/unit/providers/vector_io --ignore=tests/unit/distribution/test_library_client_initialization.py

container_recipe.notes: The --with-editable flag is critical for proper module resolution in tests. Some tests require additional dependencies from the 'unit' group that may not install cleanly in all environments (torch, qdrant-client). Core tests work without these. Full mypy check requires --group type_checking in addition to --group dev.

Integration tests require external infrastructure (Ollama, Docker containers, external APIs) and cannot be validated in isolated container
Full pre-commit validation requires Node.js (for UI linting), Go (for oasdiff), and other tools not in base Python container
Mypy with full type_checking group requires installing many type stubs and optional dependencies
The 'unit' dependency group includes many optional provider dependencies that may have installation issues in some environments
